mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a 0.5pp UNRATE rise within 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL growth periods yield positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle expansions with a t-stat above 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp surge in housing starts indicates robust consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 1.5 standard deviations above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of months where HOUST crosses the 1.5 SD threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate during inflationary periods compress equity valuations and reduce risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns remain positive in > 65% of periods following FEDFUNDS hikes during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal spending reflects immediate consumer demand strength, directly boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.
